summary: The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words were identified as merged and were split: 'እግድይዘት' into 'እግድ' and 'ይዘት'; 'እጣተክብደት' into 'እጣተ' and 'ክብደት'; and 'እጣፈንታየስደተኞቹን' into 'እጣፈንታየ' and 'ስደተኞቹን'. Minor OCR-like spelling variations, specifically the colloquial '-ለው' ending for '-ለሁ', were corrected to their standard Amharic forms (e.g., 'እጠራጠራለው' to 'እጠራጠራለሁ'). All words were successfully lemmatized to their base dictionary forms (infinitive for verbs, singular noun for nouns) and assigned a high-level Part of Speech. No words were discarded as semantic nonsense or single-character entries. The 'importance' field was uniformly set to 5 as no specific criteria for its value were provided.

The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting: 'ኦርዮንና' was split into 'ኦርዮን' (the conjunction 'ና' was discarded as per Protocol 7). 'ኦሮሚያየኦሮሚያ' was split into 'ኦሮሚያ', with the possessive form 'የኦሮሚያ' being recognized as a derivative of the same root and thus not included as a separate entry. 'ኦባብን' and 'ኦባውን' were split to their roots 'ኦባብ' and 'ኦባ' respectively, with the object marker 'ን' and definite article 'ው' being discarded. Minor OCR corrections were applied to 'ኦሮኦምኛን' which was standardized to 'ኦሮምኛ', and 'ኦስትሮጅን' was lemmatized to the more common spelling 'ኦስትሮጀን'. All words in this batch were identified as Nouns, primarily proper nouns or loanwords, and met the minimum character length requirement. No words were discarded for semantic nonsense.

summary: The processing involved several key steps. PROTOCOL 1 (THE SPLITTER) was applied to 'ከልቧና', 'ከልክሎአልና', 'ከልክሎአታልና', and 'ከልጆቿና', separating the conjunction 'ና' (and) from the preceding words. PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) led to the discarding of 'ከልታ', 'ከልነሪ', and 'ከልኮል' as they were deemed semantically meaningless or too ambiguous for reliable correction. PROTOCOL 3 (THE CORRECTOR) was used for 'ከልጆቸችዎ', which was corrected to 'ከልጆቻችሁ' (from your children, formal plural) due to a clear OCR error (double 'ች' and 'ዎ' instead of 'ሁ'). Similarly, 'ከሎሎዎቹ' was corrected to 'ከሎሌዎቹ' (from his servants), a common OCR confusion between 'ሎ' and 'ሌ'. 'ከሐሙል' was corrected to 'ከሐሙስ' (from Thursday), as 'ሐሙል' is not a recognized word. PROTOCOL 4 (THE LEMMATIZER) identified the base forms, such as 'መከልከል' for all forms of 'to forbid/prevent' and 'ልጅ' for all forms of 'child'. PROTOCOL 5 (THE TRANSLATOR) provided comprehensive English synonyms. PROTOCOL 6 (THE GRAMMARIAN) assigned high-level parts of speech. PROTOCOL 7 (THE FILTER) ensured all included words and roots were at least two characters long. All other words were processed and retained as valid Amharic entries.

Processed 30 input tokens. Discarded 'አልተንቀባረርን', 'ንገሩኋ', 'በተሳሳይ', 'እወየጡ', 'ኤደሉም', 'ያቆብን', 'ኢትዮኖፊሎሶፊ', 'ተግቢ', 'ተናከሰና' due to OCR corruption, non-standard morphology, or lack of clear semantic root. Corrected 'መፍተሔው' to 'መፍትሔ' and 'ተማቲምሽን' to 'ቲማቲም'. Split 'ሦስትመድኃኒት' into two distinct entities.

summary: The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. PROTOCOL 1 (The Splitter) identified and separated merged words: 'አያስፈልግምይህ' into 'አያስፈልግም' and 'ይህ', and 'አያስፈልግምፌደራላዊውን' into 'አያስፈልግም' and 'ፌደራላዊውን'. PROTOCOL 2 (The Judge) found all words to be semantically valid; no words were discarded for being nonsense. PROTOCOL 3 (The Corrector) was applied to 'አያስፈራውምም' and 'አያሸትቱምም', where the redundant emphatic 'ም' particle was stripped to yield cleaner forms 'አያስፈራውም' and 'አያሸትቱም' respectively, ensuring the core verb was captured. PROTOCOL 4 (The Lemmatizer) derived the infinitive roots for all verbs, such as 'ማስወንጀል' for 'አያስወነጅልም' and 'ማስፈራት' for 'አያስፈራኝ'. For 'አያስፈለግም', the root 'መፈለግ' was chosen, recognizing its passive construction. For 'አያቋርጤነት', the word itself serves as its own noun root. PROTOCOL 5 (The Translator) provided comprehensive English synonyms. PROTOCOL 6 (The Grammarian) assigned high-level parts of speech (Verb, Pronoun, Adjective). PROTOCOL 7 (The Filter) confirmed all processed words and roots were at least two characters long; no single-character words were included. No words were discarded entirely from the batch.

summary: The batch of Amharic words was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. No words required splitting (PROTOCOL 1). Two words were discarded under PROTOCOL 2 (Semantic Filtering) and PROTOCOL 3 (Corrector): 'አይለድቅል' was deemed semantically meaningless as it does not correspond to any known Amharic root or word formation. 'አይመስረት' was discarded because it is not a standard or recognizable verb conjugation; while 'መመስረት' (to found) exists, 'አይመስረት' is ambiguous and cannot be corrected with certainty without guessing. All remaining words were successfully lemmatized to their infinitive forms (PROTOCOL 4), provided with comprehensive English synonyms (PROTOCOL 5), and assigned a single part of speech (PROTOCOL 6). All included words and their roots met the minimum two-character length requirement (PROTOCOL 7).

summary:
The batch of Amharic words underwent an 8-protocol scholarly analysis. Several words required splitting due to being merged, including 'አየረንብረቷ' (split into 'አየሩ' and 'ንብረቷ'), 'አየራይንፎቶ' (split into 'አየር', 'አይን', 'ፎቶ'), 'አየርንና' (split into 'አየርን' and 'ና'), 'አየርየተሻለ' (split into 'አየር' and 'የተሻለ'), 'አየርጎርፍ' (split into 'አየር' and 'ጎርፍ'), 'አየስተያየት' (split into 'አየ' and 'አስተያየት'), 'አየቀረበ' (split into 'አየ' and 'ቀረበ'), 'አየቀረቡበት' (split into 'አየ' and 'ቀረቡበት'), 'አየቆየ' (split into 'አየ' and 'ቆየ'), 'አየተመለሰ' (split into 'አየ' and 'ተመለሰ'), 'አየተሰደብንና' (split into 'አየ', 'ተሰደብን', and 'ና'), and 'አየተሸረሸረ' (split into 'አየ' and 'ተሸረሸረ').

One word, 'አየብም', was discarded entirely as it presented a meaningless combination of characters that could not be reliably corrected or assigned semantic value, adhering to PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE).

Scholarly guesses and corrections were applied to address minor OCR errors and ambiguities: 'አየረ' in 'አየረንብረቷ' was corrected to 'አየሩ' (the air) based on contextual likelihood. Similarly, 'አየሮ' was corrected to 'አየሩ' (the air). 'ስተያየት' in 'አየስተያየት' was corrected to 'አስተያየት' (opinion) due to a clear missing initial 'አ'. The word 'አዝጠጋ' was analyzed as a valid, albeit less common, causative form of 'ጠጋ' (to approach), rather than an OCR error for 'አስጠጋ', as the 'ዝ' character was distinct and a causative morphology was plausible. The conjunction 'ና' was consistently lemmatized to 'እና'.

summary: The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. One word, 'አይብሃለው', was discarded under PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) as it presented as semantically nonsensical and too ambiguous for a reliable correction. A scholarly guess was made for 'አይቤዠውም', correcting it to 'አይበዠውም' (from 'መበዠት') based on common OCR errors and standard Amharic verb morphology. A significant observation was made regarding words starting with 'አይተ-' or 'አይታ-'. While 'አይ' typically denotes a negative future tense, in many instances within this batch (e.g., 'አይተናት', 'አይተኸዋልም', 'አይተውማል', 'አይታችሁኝ'), the 'አይ' is an integral part of the past tense conjugation of the verb 'ማየት' (to see), not a separate negative prefix. This distinction was crucial for accurate lemmatization and translation. All other words were successfully cleaned, lemmatized, translated, and tagged according to the specified protocols, ensuring no single-character words or roots were included.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words were identified as merged and split accordingly: 'አይገለበጥምና' was split into 'አይገለበጥም' and 'እና'; 'አይገባምናአላት' into 'አይገባምና' and 'አላት'; 'አይገባብሽምና' into 'አይገባብሽም' and 'እና'; 'አይገባቸውምና' into 'አይገባቸውም' and 'እና'; 'አይገባኝምና' into 'አይገባኝም' and 'እና'; 'አይገኙምና' into 'አይገኙም' and 'እና'; 'አይገኙምከቡሽ' into 'አይገኙም', 'ከ', and 'ቡሽ'; 'አይገዙምና' into 'አይገዙም' and 'እና'; 'አይገዛምና' into 'አይገዛም' and 'እና'; and 'አይገዛችሁምና' into 'አይገዛችሁም' and 'እና'.

Three words were discarded due to semantic nonsense or extreme ambiguity (PROTOCOL 2 & 3): 'አይዳቢያ', 'አይገቴ', and 'አይገዜ'. These strings do not correspond to known Amharic words or common morphological patterns, and no certain correction could be made. All remaining words were successfully lemmatized, translated, and tagged with their appropriate part of speech. No single-character words were discarded from the valid splits.

summary: The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. One word, 'አይንየኢትዮጵያ', was split into two distinct words: 'አይን' (eye) and 'የኢትዮጵያ' (of Ethiopia), as per PROTOCOL 1 (THE SPLITTER). Two words, 'አይከፍቴ' and 'አይኪያ', were discarded under PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) and PROTOCOL 3 (THE CORRECTOR) because they were deemed semantically ambiguous or meaningless in Amharic without further context, and no certain correction could be applied. Acronyms 'አይኢኤስ' and 'አይኤስአይኤስን' were identified as Proper Nouns, with their roots being the acronyms themselves, as traditional Amharic lemmatization does not apply. All other words were successfully lemmatized, translated, and tagged according to the specified protocols.
